reading1.title: FAMILY RELATIONSHIPS IN VIETNAM
reading1.passage:
Family relationships in Vietnam differ in several ways from those in many Western countries. Most Vietnamese households are extended, with several generations living close together and sharing childcare and household duties. Within this extended family, the oldest family member typically holds the greatest authority and takes charge of key domestic decisions. Each family follows its own rules and values: younger members are expected to show respect, while elders are encouraged to act with patience and kindness toward the younger generation. These traditional values still shape relations not only between parents and children but also between husband and wife.

Shared meals remain a powerful symbol of togetherness. Traditionally, the whole extended family would eat on the floor, though many households now use dining tables while keeping the same sense of intimacy. Influenced by Confucian ideals, Vietnamese mothers play a central role, often associated with love, sacrifice, and kindness. Children are widely regarded as the family's greatest treasure, and parents typically apply clear, sometimes strict rules to develop good behavior and responsibility. Although modern economic pressures have led many families - especially in cities - to have fewer children than in the past, the proverb "Blood is thicker than water" still resonates. It means that family relationships are the most important. Family ties are very strong in Vietnam.

Vietnamese families have unique traditional values and customs.

reading2.title: HA LONG BAY: A NATURAL WONDER
reading2.passage:
Ha Long Bay, situated in Quang Ninh Province, is recognized worldwide as one of Vietnam's most renowned and breathtaking natural wonders. Due to its unparalleled geological and aesthetic value, it has been officially recognized twice by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site. The bay is visually defined by its thousands of spectacular limestone karsts and isles that rise majestically from the water, appearing in various shapes and sizes, often described as floating sculptures. The evocative name "Ha Long" itself translates to "descending dragon," a reference originating from a captivating local legend about dragons sent to protect Vietnam.

Visitors to Ha Long Bay typically choose to embark on a cruise to fully admire the magnificent scenery. Popular activities available to tourists include kayaking around the karsts, swimming in secluded coves, and exploring famous caves like the vast Sung Sot Cave or the smaller Thien Cung Cave. The surrounding water is a distinct emerald green, contributing to a uniquely tranquil and peaceful atmosphere.

Apart from its dramatic natural beauty, Ha Long Bay is also historically and culturally important. It is home to traditional floating villages where generations of fishermen have lived and maintained a unique way of life. Tourists are often given the opportunity to visit these communities to learn about the local people's specialized culture. To ensure this irreplaceable environment remains a beautiful destination for both tourists and residents, preserving the bay's environment is now considered a fundamental top priority for all stakeholders and future generations.

opts: Ha Long Bay: A Natural Wonder

His handmade products are popular, being sold not only in the local market but also to the tourists who visit the village. Mr. Ba is deeply proud of his job because it helps preserve the traditional craft and cultural heritage of his hometown. Furthermore, he is dedicated to the future of the craft. He actively teaches the young people in the village how to make bamboo baskets. This ensures they can earn a living and, critically, keep the tradition alive for generations to come.

reading2.title: HEALTHY LIFESTYLE FOR TEENS
reading2.passage:
For teenagers, staying healthy is crucial for their growth and development. A healthy lifestyle includes a balanced diet, regular exercise, and enough sleep.

First, a balanced diet provides the body with the necessary nutrients. Teens should eat plenty of fruits, vegetables, and whole grains while limiting sugary snacks and fast food. Drinking enough water is also vital to keep the body hydrated.

Second, physical activity is essential. Experts recommend that teens get at least 60 minutes of moderate to vigorous exercise every day. This can include sports, dancing, or simply walking or cycling to school. Exercise not only helps teens grow taller, but also builds strong bones and muscles and reduces stress.

Finally, sleep plays a key role in mental and physical health. Teenagers need about 8 to 10 hours of sleep per night to function effectively. Lack of sleep can lead to poor concentration at school and mood swings.

By following these simple habits, teenagers can maintain good health and perform better in their daily activities.

reading1.title: LIFE IN VIETNAMESE VILLAGES IN THE PAST
reading1.passage:
Decades ago, life in Vietnamese villages was notably simple and peaceful. There was no electricity, so people relied on oil lamps for light after sunset. Evenings were often spent gathering at the communal house, where villagers would share stories, talk, or listen to the radio together. Children did not have access to modern gadgets like iPads or smartphones. Instead, their childhood was filled with traditional games such as hide-and-seek, flying kites, or skipping rope, often played outdoors in the fields.

Despite the hardship and lack of modern facilities, the community spirit was very strong. People were incredibly friendly and helpful. Neighbors had deep bonds, treating one another like family. If a family faced any difficulty, the whole village would immediately come to their aid. Today, life has significantly changed with modern technology and facilities, bringing new comforts. However, many elderly people still look back on the past with great nostalgia for its warmth and close-knit relationships.

opts: Life in Vietnamese villages in the past

reading2.title: THE AO DAI: VIETNAMESE TRADITIONAL DRESS
reading2.passage:
The "Ao Dai" is recognized worldwide as the graceful traditional dress of Vietnamese women. The garment typically consists of a long, flowing silk tunic that is elegantly slit on the sides from the waist down, worn over a pair of loose-fitting trousers. Historically, the Ao Dai was worn by both men and women. However, over time, its design has evolved, and in modern Vietnam, it is primarily worn by women, usually reserved for special occasions such as weddings, Tet (Lunar New Year), or formal school uniforms.

The Ao Dai is not a fixed design; it has undergone numerous changes throughout the years. For instance, during the 1960s and 1970s, the trend saw the rise of the "mini Ao Dai," which featured a shorter length and tighter fit, appealing to a younger generation. Nowadays, contemporary fashion designers continue to modernize the Ao Dai. They experiment with a wider variety of materials, from traditional silk to more modern, breathable fabrics, and introduce new patterns and colors. The goal of these changes is to make the Ao Dai more versatile and comfortable, suitable not just for festivals and ceremonies but potentially for daily life as well. Despite all these adaptations, the Ao Dai has maintained its profound role as an iconic symbol of Vietnamese beauty, culture, and national identity. It represents the elegance, modesty, and grace of Vietnamese women.

questions.q: What is the best title for the passage?
opts: The Ao Dai: Vietnamese Traditional Dress

reading1.title: ELECTRONIC DEVICES: BENEFITS AND DRAWBACKS
reading1.passage:
Electronic devices such as smartphones, tablets, and laptops have become an essential part of our lives. They primarily serve to help us communicate instantly, work efficiently, and entertain ourselves through various applications. For students, these devices are undeniably useful tools for learning. They provide easy access to online dictionaries, allow students to watch countless educational videos, and enable them to join virtual classrooms, expanding their knowledge beyond traditional textbooks.

However, using electronic devices too much can have significant negative effects. Staring continuously at screens for long hours can lead to serious health issues, most commonly causing eye strain and painful headaches. Furthermore, excessive screen time can make people lazy because they tend to spend far less time engaging in important physical activities and outdoor recreation. Therefore, it is critically important for everyone to use these powerful devices reasonably and moderately to enjoy the benefits while effectively avoiding potential health problems.

reading2.title: PLANET EARTH: FEATURES AND THREATS
reading2.passage:
Planet Earth holds a unique place in the cosmos, being the only known planet in the universe confirmed to support life. It is famously known as the "Blue Planet" because approximately 71% of its surface is covered by vast oceans and other bodies of water, giving it a distinct appearance from space. Essential for sustaining life, the Earth's atmosphere contains abundant oxygen, a gas that is vital for humans and animals alike to breathe and survive.

Despite its unique life-sustaining features, our beloved planet is currently facing many serious problems. The most significant challenge is climate change, primarily caused by the buildup of greenhouse gases from human activities. This pollution is making the Earth progressively warmer, leading to dire consequences such as accelerated melting of polar ice caps, dangerous sea-level rise, and an increase in devastating extreme weather events. Furthermore, widespread deforestation and general pollution are continually destroying the natural habitats of countless species, pushing them towards extinction.

To successfully protect our planet, we need to take immediate and collective action. We must urgently reduce our carbon footprint by transitioning to renewable energy sources such as solar and wind power. Planting more trees to absorb carbon dioxide and making an effort to recycle waste are simple yet highly effective ways for every individual to help. Saving the Earth is truly the fundamental responsibility of every person living on it.

reading1.title: PUBLIC TRANSPORT AND CITY DEVELOPMENT
reading1.passage:
Public transport is an essential part of modern city development. It provides people with a convenient, safe, and affordable way to travel while helping reduce traffic congestion and air pollution. Well-organized systems of buses, trains, and metro lines make cities more efficient, comfortable, and sustainable places to live.

In major cities like Seoul or London, public transport plays a vital role in people's daily lives. It enables millions of passengers to move quickly from one area to another without using private cars. This helps lower fuel consumption and protects the environment from harmful emissions. Moreover, public transport promotes social equality by giving everyone, including students, workers, and the elderly, equal access to education, employment, and recreation.

However, many cities still face serious challenges such as overcrowded buses, frequent delays, and outdated infrastructure. These problems can make the system less reliable, less comfortable, and less attractive to passengers. To deal with them, governments should invest more in upgrading vehicles, expanding routes, improving safety standards, and ensuring better cleanliness and punctuality.

In conclusion, public transport brings numerous social and environmental benefits. Encouraging citizens to use it more often is an effective way to create cleaner, greener, and more sustainable urban environments for future generations.

reading2.title: SINGAPORE
reading2.passage:
Singapore, a dynamic island nation in Southeast Asia, is a remarkable example of cultural harmony and rapid modernization. While geographically small, its influence is enormous. The city-state is a genuine melting pot, with a population comprised mainly of Chinese, Malay, and Indian ethnic groups. This blend is immediately apparent in its cuisine, which is famous worldwide for its diverse flavors and styles, ranging from Malay satay to Indian curry and Chinese Hainanese chicken rice. This cultural mixture is formalized by four official languages: English, Malay, Mandarin Chinese, and Tamil. This multi-lingual environment is key to the country's success as a global trade hub.

Beyond its cultural dynamism, Singapore is globally celebrated for its futuristic urban planning. Iconic structures like the Supertree Grove at Gardens by the Bay and the stunning architecture of the Marina Bay Sands reflect a commitment to innovation and sustainability. Despite its dense population, the government places a high priority on green spaces, ensuring that the city is filled with parks and trees. Tourists visiting the city are consistently impressed by the efficiency of the public transport system and the low crime rate. The nation's ability to maintain order and cleanliness while embracing global influences makes it a unique experience for any visitor.

reading1.title: LIVING GREEN
reading1.passage:
The most obvious advantage of living green is that it helps reduce environmental pollution. This is done by practising the 3Rs. The first R means choosing products very carefully to reduce waste as much as possible. Reusing involves using items repeatedly. The third R is about separating materials that can be recycled and later used for a new purpose.

There are also economic benefits to living green. Reducing energy and water use can help save money on household bills. Furthermore, reusing products and materials is another way to save. It helps reduce production costs, since creating new products wastes materials and is expensive. Recycled products also last longer than new ones, making them a better and more eco-friendly option.

Lastly, going green offers health benefits. It reduces air pollutants by making the air we breathe cleaner and healthier. This means fewer diseases and doctor's appointments. Another way to improve your health is to purchase organic foods and green products for your household. They don't use harmful chemicals that can lead to health issues. In turn, you are also supporting responsible farming methods that protect the environment.

reading2.title: ELECTRONIC DEVICES
reading2.passage:
In the modern world, electronic devices like smartphones, tablets, and laptops have become indispensable. They offer numerous benefits, especially for students. Firstly, they provide instant access to vast amounts of educational resources and allow students to connect with teachers and peers easily, making distance learning possible. Secondly, these devices are excellent tools for organization, helping users manage their schedules and keep track of deadlines. However, the pervasive use of these devices also comes with significant drawbacks.

The most common problem is digital addiction, where users spend excessive time online, leading to reduced focus and social isolation. Prolonged screen use can also cause eye strain and disrupt sleep patterns. Parents and schools should therefore teach students to use technology responsibly and encourage them to set strict boundaries on screen time to maximize benefits while minimizing negative impacts.

reading1.title: MODERN TECHNOLOGY AND ITS IMPACT
reading1.passage:
Modern technology has profoundly shaped how we live and work. Devices like smartphones and smartwatches now manage many aspects of our daily routines, from tracking fitness goals to scheduling appointments. For communication, we rely on instant messaging and video calls, making global contact effortless and immediate. This connectivity has broken down geographical barriers, fostering greater international cooperation. In education, technology offers personalized learning experiences and access to vast online libraries, allowing students worldwide to learn at their own pace. Furthermore, in the workplace, technology has automated many tasks, significantly boosting productivity across various industries.

However, this convenience comes with trade-offs. The constant connection can lead to digital fatigue and increase stress levels as people feel pressured to be available 24/7. Furthermore, there are growing concerns about data privacy and security, as our personal information is increasingly collected and stored online, leaving us vulnerable to cyber threats. To truly benefit from technology, individuals must learn to use it mindfully, setting clear boundaries to protect their mental well-being and privacy.

reading2.title: NATURAL DISASTERS AND PREPAREDNESS
reading2.passage:
Natural disasters, such as floods, droughts, and typhoons, pose a severe threat to communities worldwide. Their frequency and intensity are increasing due to Climate Change, a long-term shift in global weather patterns.

A drought, for example, is a prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall that severely impacts agriculture, ruins crops, and leads to food shortages. This lack of water also damages local economies and affects public health. Conversely, typhoons bring heavy rain and strong winds, causing widespread flooding and destruction to coastal areas and infrastructure. Coastal communities often face the challenge of rebuilding after they strike.

Preparing for these disasters is essential for minimizing loss of life and property. Communities should establish robust early warning systems and regularly practice evacuation drills. Furthermore, governments need to invest in resilient infrastructure that can withstand extreme weather events. Individuals, on their part, should prepare emergency kits containing food, water, and first-aid supplies. Following official instructions is crucial during a disaster, as these guidelines provide the safest routes and shelters. By investing in resilient infrastructure and consistently educating the public, we can significantly reduce the risks and damage caused by these destructive natural events.

reading1.title: ROBOTICS IN MODERN SCIENCE
reading1.passage:
Robotics is one of the fastest-growing and most transformative fields in modern science and engineering. Robots are essentially programmable machines capable of performing complex tasks automatically and with high accuracy. They are increasingly used in various sectors globally, extending from heavy manufacturing and consumer goods production to delicate healthcare procedures and deep space exploration. In factories, the key benefit of robots is that they enhance efficiency and precision, often performing dangerous or monotonous, repetitive jobs that are unsuitable for humans. In medicine, specifically, robotic surgery allows doctors to perform complex, minimally invasive procedures with greater accuracy, leading to faster patient recovery.

However, the rapid advancement of robotics raises significant ethical questions about societal impacts, primarily concerning job displacement and the changing future role of human labor. To address safety concerns and facilitate better human-robot collaboration, researchers are currently working on developing "soft robots" that can safely interact with humans and adapt fluidly to changing environments. The future of science will undoubtedly be defined by its integration with robotics.

reading2.title: ENVIRONMENTAL CRISIS
reading2.passage:
Our planet is currently facing an unprecedented environmental crisis, one primarily driven and accelerated by decades of human activities. The most significant and overarching challenge is climate change, caused by the continuous buildup of greenhouse gases, primarily carbon dioxide, in the Earth's atmosphere. These atmospheric blankets trap heat, leading to a progressive and dangerous warming trend known as the greenhouse effect.

The consequences are truly dire: we are witnessing the accelerated melting of polar ice caps and glaciers, leading to dangerous sea level rise that threatens coastal cities and small island nations. Furthermore, the planet is experiencing an alarming increase in devastating extreme weather events such as stronger typhoons, prolonged heatwaves, and longer, more intense droughts.

Beyond climate change, widespread deforestation for agriculture and logging, coupled with extensive plastic pollution in oceans and landfills, are continually destroying the natural habitats of countless species, pushing them towards extinction. To effectively address this complex crisis, global cooperation among nations is vital. However, individuals also play a critical role by reducing their waste, conserving water and energy, and choosing sustainable consumption patterns. Protecting the environment is not just a scientific or political issue; it is a collective and shared responsibility for the health and future stability of our planet.

reading1.title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SLEEP FOR TEENAGERS
reading1.passage:
Sleep plays an important role in teens' overall health and well-being. During sleep, the body undergoes essential processes, such as growth, repair, and restoration. Adequate sleep has been linked to improved cognitive function, memory, and concentration, which are vital for academic performance. It also boosts mood and emotional well-being, reducing the risk of mental health issues, such as depression and anxiety.

Teens need 8-10 hours of sleep per night, but many fall short due to factors like schoolwork, extracurricular activities, and electronic devices. Lack of sleep can have negative effects on teens, leading to reduced attention span, poor decision-making, and an increased risk of accidents. Moreover, lack of sleep has been associated with obesity, as it disrupts hormone regulation and increases cravings for unhealthy foods. It can also weaken the immune system, making teens more susceptible to illnesses.

To ensure a good night's sleep, teens should plan a consistent sleep schedule, make a sleep-friendly environment, limit caffeine and electronic device use before bedtime, and engage in relaxation techniques. Parents and educators play a crucial role in promoting healthy sleep habits and raising awareness of the importance of sleep for teens' overall well-being.

reading2.passage:
Jobs Of The Future: What Will Our Children Be Doing In 2040?
Many jobs today didn't exist 10 or 20 years ago. In the future, work will change even more because of new technology. Professor Carl Frey, an expert from Oxford University, says students should gain necessary skills now to be ready for new jobs by 2040.

Here are some future jobs:
Renewable Energy Scientist: These scientists will help make clean energy like wind and solar power. Students should study science and maths to prepare.
Virtual Reality Developer: People may work in the metaverse, a digital world. Developers will write computer code to build these worlds. Students should learn ICT, maths, and science.
Virtual Reality Artist: These artists use technology to create digital art that appears in real places. Subjects like art, ICT, and design are helpful.
Yoga Instructor: Some jobs will still focus on real human contact. Yoga is one of them.
Health Chef: In the future, chefs may cook food to improve health. Food technology and science are valuable for this job.

Even with all these changes, Professor Frey says students should still learn subjects that they have traditionally been focusing on, as they are still very important and useful for future careers.

reading1.title: TRAVELING IN THE PAST AND NOW
reading1.passage:
In the past, traveling was nothing less than a dangerous adventure. People had no means of transport as they do now. They faced all sorts of dangers, including bad weather, wild beasts, and robbers. Therefore, most people did not want to leave their villages. Only those who were adventurous set out on a long journey.

When man succeeded in training the four-footed animals like the horse and the camel, traveling became fairly comfortable. A trip across the country was still a dangerous undertaking. In those days, it was also very difficult to transport goods from one place to another. A man carried his load on his head or back. Later, horses and other animals were used for this purpose. But neither man nor animals could carry very heavy loads from one place to another.

Now, people living in any part of a country can travel to any other part in comfort. Every country sets up a railway network. A small sum of money is required to go from one corner of the country to another. If your business is more urgent, you can travel by airplane. A plane will need only a couple of days to fly around the whole world. Traveling by sea has also become swifter, more comfortable and secure than before.

reading2.title: HOW TO BECOME AN ACTOR
reading2.passage:
If you have decided to become a doctor or an engineer, it's easy to find information on how to become one. It's more difficult if you want to be an actor. Nobody can say, "First you do this, then you do that, and one day you're an actor". But I can tell you some things that will help.

Learning to be an actor usually starts at school. Drama lessons and clubs can teach students many things, including different ways of acting and how to write their own plays. Writing helps you understand a lot about acting. But even more important is learning to work in a group with other actors.

However, school lessons and clubs are only one part of learning about being an actor. Find small theatre groups near where you live - and don't worry about being the star! It doesn't matter if you only have one word to say in a play; it will still help you improve. You need to be on stage as much as possible, in a variety of plays.

When you finish school, you should think about studying at university. Of course, it's possible to be an actor without going to university, but doing a theatre course is a good idea for some people. These courses may improve your acting, and also help you learn about a variety of subjects, such as business, history, music and dance.

Every day on the radio, TV, and in the newspaper, we hear, see, or read about many problems in the world, such as pollution.

Air pollution is the first kind. It mostly comes from fumes released from motorbikes, cars, airplanes, trains and poisonous gases emitted from factories. Also, waste is dumped anywhere, even in the city where many people live. The second pollution problem is sea pollution. Many people earn their living from fishing in the sea, and the fish they catch feed many people. But the sea has become so polluted from oil spills and factory wastes that the fish are dying. This not only kills the fish but also affects people who eat fish.

Seldom do you find a place nowadays that is not polluted. This problem is growing more difficult every day. We must find a good solution that makes the world a better place to live.

reading2.passage:
Paper was used by ancient Egyptians as writing material more than 5000 years ago. The Chinese invented paper 2000 years ago.

A piece of paper is really made up of tiny fibers. The fibers used in paper are plant fibers, and there are millions of them in one sheet. In addition to plant fiber, dyes and additives, such as resin, may be used. Dyes can color the paper; resin may add weight and texture.

Where do these fibers come from? The majority of paper is made from the plant fiber that comes from trees. Millions of trees are cut down, but new trees are planted in their places. Wastepaper that has been made and used can be turned into recycled paper. This recycling process saves forests, energy, and reduces air and water pollution.

reading1.title: TRAVEL ADVICE
reading1.passage: The price of holidays can fluctuate a great deal throughout the year, so you can save a lot if you are flexible with your travel dates and avoid peak holiday times. It can also be cheaper if you book well in advance. Before your departure, make sure you get as much information about your destination as you can. Find out if you require any special visas or permits to travel there. Think about spending money as well. Will you be able to access your own money easily enough or will you need to take cash with you? Think about eating larger lunches and smaller evening meals to help your money go further, since lunch is generally cheaper. Make sure that you keep sufficient identification with you at all times. It may also help to email a copy of your passport details to yourself, in case it is lost or stolen. Label your suitcases clearly so that they can be easily identified as yours. It can be useful to store a copy of your itinerary in a prominent place in your suitcase so the airline knows where to find you if your luggage is lost. Be sure to pack any medication or other essential items in your hand luggage. If your flight is delayed, or your luggage is lost, these can be difficult to obtain in an airport or a foreign country.

reading2.title: COMPUTER HACKING AND ONLINE SAFETY
reading2.passage:
One of the biggest dangers on the Internet today is computer hacking. Hackers are people who enter computer systems illegally. They may change or delete information, steal personal data, or spread viruses that can damage files. These actions can cause serious problems for users and organizations.

Many hackers use something called protocols to get into computer systems. Protocols are rules built into computer software. They help computers communicate with each other. When one computer connects to another, the protocols check if the connection is allowed and how much information can be shared. Hackers can change these protocols so they can enter a system without permission.

There are two main types of hackers. The first type is called passive hackers. They enjoy entering difficult systems, such as bank or military networks, but they usually do not cause damage. They feel excited just because they can access the system.

The second type is active hackers. These hackers are more dangerous. After entering a system, they may delete files, steal information, or spread viruses. Their actions can seriously harm computer systems and users.

The best way to protect a computer system is to use a strong password. Long and unusual passwords are harder to guess. Some websites also use a "password-plus" system. This means users must enter a password and a second code. The code changes each time. This makes the system safer because hackers need both the password and the code to get access.

reading1.title: PHU QUOC ISLAND
reading1.passage:
After only a 50-minute flight from Ho Chi Minh City, you will set foot on the tropical paradise of Vietnam and the world: Phu Quoc Island.

The island has a roughly triangular shape with a north-south length of 50km and a west-east width of 25km. A great part of the terrain is filled with beautiful sandy beaches, but there is a mountainous region with 99 peaks, among which the Peak of Chua Mountain is the tallest at 603 meters. Due to Phu Quoc's location in the Gulf of Thailand, its climate is sub-equatorial, with temperate weather year-round, making trips to Phu Quoc possible any time of year. However, the best time to travel to this island is during the dry season, from November to March, when the sky is clear and the rains are away.

Phu Quoc is most famous for its cuisine and its wonderful natural coastline. The most famous food of Phu Quoc is fish sauce, which has become quite popular all over Vietnam and the world. Besides, another thing worth trying there is the spicy yet interesting black pepper. However, the factor that will definitely intrigue you to visit Phu Quoc is its untouched coastline featuring several heavenly beaches. They have yet to be explored to their full ability, but this fact might actually be positive, as it gives the shores a romantic beauty that you can find nowhere else in the world.

reading2.title: CELL PHONES IN EDUCATION
reading2.passage:
Cell phones are an integral part of our society and their main use is communication. They keep students in touch with the rest of the world by giving them the power to interact with it. In my day, if you forgot your lunch, you were at the mercy of the office calling home for you. Now, students have the ability to solve their own problems and handle certain emergencies on their own.

Cell phones also allow students to stay in touch with classmates at other schools or friends who don't attend school. While not an educational benefit directly, better relationships can lead to higher self-esteem and reduce isolation, which is good for everybody. In the same way, camera phones allow students to capture the kinds of memories that help build a solid school culture, and, in some cases, can act as documentation of misbehavior in the same way that store cameras provide evidence and deter bad behavior.

Academically, the cell phone can be used to record a procedure or explanation that may need to be reviewed later. It could also be used to record a lecture for later review. And just imagine if classes could be easily taped for students who are absent? What if they could even be streamed and seen from home instantly?

reading1.title: RECYCLING METAL CANS
reading1.passage:
In the UK, we each use about two hundred metal food and drink cans every year. Metal cans are popular because they are convenient, easy to store and unbreakable. But when you have finished with a can, what do you do with it? Do you throw it away and forget all about it?

Probably! But behind the scenes, there are people whose job it is to make sure that the metal is never wasted. In fact, recycling or reusing metal cans is so successful that every day of the year, more than five million cans start new lives in new metal products.

After you have thrown away your can, what happens? Well, first of all, it is collected by the men who empty your dustbin each week and taken to a landfill, together with all the other household rubbish. Then the rubbish is sorted, and the metal cans are taken separately to a special factory where they find their way back into your home in the form of knives and forks, garden equipment, and, of course, food and drink cans. So the next time you open your soft drink, just remember where your can may have been!

opts: How the soft drink industry works.

reading2.title: JOBS AND INNOVATIONS
reading2.passage:
It seems difficult to see the true link between innovations and job opportunities. In practice, the connection can be quite strong. For example, with the introduction of virtual reality (VR), there is now a high demand for professional engineers in the entertainment business. They are in charge of designing, developing and testing entertainment products.

The world of technology changes every day. This means we can expect new job opportunities across every field. Podcasts are already popular, and everyone can be a producer. A podcast can be audio or video content that a producer uploads to social media using the internet. Newer technology will make it easier. To keep listeners or audiences entertained, the content must be professional. This means demand for skilled content writers and producers will increase.

Data scientists will be a key term in job searches for companies or producers in the media industry. To attract more audiences, they need to understand what their audiences expect so that they can produce more personalized content. To do that, a producer or company needs a lot of information about the audience's interests.

And that's what a data scientist is for.

However, it's important to note that new job opportunities also require new skills. It's best to learn the necessary skills so that you can keep up with these changes.

reading1.title: BRAZIL
reading1.passage:
Brazil, the largest country in South America and the fifth-largest in the world, is located in the eastern half of the continent. The people of Brazil are famous for their outgoing, friendly, and fun-loving nature. Brazilian women are considered to be among the best-dressed in the world. Brazil is a federal republic with 23 states. The capital city is Brasilia. Portuguese is the official language and it is spoken with a distinct Brazilian accent. Brazil has a population of over 138 million people of many different races and ethnic groups.

A number of industrial products are produced in Brazil, including cars, chemicals, ships, machines, and military weapons. Mining is also an important industry. Agriculture also plays a very important role in the economic development of this country. Many crops, including coffee (Brazil is the world's largest coffee producer), cotton, soybeans, sugar, cocoa, rice, corn, and fruit, are exported.

reading2.title: HOW TO LEARN ENGLISH EFFECTIVELY
reading2.passage:
English is one of the most popular languages in the world. It comes second in the number of speakers after Chinese. In Vietnam, more and more people are studying English and they consider it a key to success. However, not everyone knows how to learn English effectively. The best way to improve the four skills: speaking, listening, reading, and writing, is to practice regularly. You should make use of every opportunity to speak English with friends in class or at English-speaking clubs or practice ourselves in front of the mirror. Learning all the words by heart does not help much if you do not read a lot, because you will easily forget what you have learnt.

Reading books, listening to the radio and watching films are better ways to memorize words. Besides, English learners should not be so shy, as making mistakes is unavoidable in learning a foreign language. Practicing speaking a lot is a good way to correct your mistakes. There is no need to worry about not having a native-English accent. It's more important to be able to speak clearly, so that people can understand us.

Another way to improve the language is to use a dictionary. An English dictionary helps us a lot by showing the pronunciation of every word and which part of the word is stressed. Learners can also exploit various meanings of a word and look for the synonyms and antonyms.
